z:Pen recording operations that can be accessed or replayed.
print_function
division
absolute_import)
AbstractPen
DecomposingPen)
replayRecording
RecordingPen
DecomposingRecordingPenc
Replay a recording, as produced by RecordingPen or DecomposingRecordingPen,
	to a pen.
	Note that recording does not have to be produced by those pens.
	It can be any iterable of tuples of method name and tuple-of-arguments.
	Likewise, pen can be any objects receiving those method calls.
getattr)
Z	recording
operatorZ
operands
?/usr/lib/python3.9/site-packages/fontTools/pens/recordingPen.pyr
Pen recording operations that can be accessed or replayed.
	The recording can be accessed as pen.value; or replayed using
	pen.replay(otherPen).
	Usage example:
	==============
	from fontTools.ttLib import TTFont
	from fontTools.pens.recordingPen import RecordingPen
	glyph_name = 'dollar'
	font_path = 'MyFont.otf'
	font = TTFont(font_path)
	glyphset = font.getGlyphSet()
	glyph = glyphset[glyph_name]
	pen = RecordingPen()
	glyph.draw(pen)
	print(pen.value)
value
selfr

RecordingPen.__init__c
moveTo
append)
RecordingPen.moveToc
lineTor
RecordingPen.lineToc
qCurveTor
pointsr
RecordingPen.qCurveToc
curveTor
RecordingPen.curveToc
	closePathr
RecordingPen.closePathc
endPathr
RecordingPen.endPathc
addComponentr
Z	glyphNameZ
transformationr
RecordingPen.addComponentc
replay=
RecordingPen.replayN)
__name__
__module__
__qualname__
__doc__r
 Same as RecordingPen, except that it doesn't keep components
	as references, but draws them decomposed as regular contours.
	The constructor takes a single 'glyphSet' positional argument,
	a dictionary of glyph objects (i.e. with a 'draw' method) keyed
	by thir name.
	>>> class SimpleGlyph(object):
	...     def draw(self, pen):
	...         pen.moveTo((0, 0))
	...         pen.curveTo((1, 1), (2, 2), (3, 3))
	...         pen.closePath()
	>>> class CompositeGlyph(object):
	...     def draw(self, pen):
	...         pen.addComponent('a', (1, 0, 0, 1, -1, 1))
	>>> glyphSet = {'a': SimpleGlyph(), 'b': CompositeGlyph()}
	>>> for name, glyph in sorted(glyphSet.items()):
	...     pen = DecomposingRecordingPen(glyphSet)
	...     glyph.draw(pen)
	...     print("{}: {}".format(name, pen.value))
	a: [('moveTo', ((0, 0),)), ('curveTo', ((1, 1), (2, 2), (3, 3))), ('closePath', ())]
	b: [('moveTo', ((-1, 1),)), ('curveTo', ((0, 2), (1, 3), (2, 4))), ('closePath', ())]
	FN)
skipMissingComponentsr
__main__)
_TestPen)
pprintN)
__future__r
fontTools.misc.py23Z
fontTools.pens.basePenr
__all__r
<module>
